sanskrit: यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते। निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा॥
transliteration: yadā viniyataṃ cittam ātmany evāvatiṣṭhate | niḥspṛhaḥ sarva-kāmebhyo yukta ity ucyate tadā ||
literal_meaning: When the disciplined mind is established in the Self alone, free from all desires, then one is said to be ‘yoked’ (yukta).
contextual_meaning: The verse marks the tipping-point where attention no longer chases objects but rests in its own source, collapsing the compulsive feedback loop between craving and gratification.
psychological_state: Absence of craving-driven arousal; mind functions as a calm mirror rather than a magnet.
trigger_scenario: You close every shopping app on your phone, feel no pull to reopen them, and notice the quiet space that remains.
anchor_text: Rest the mind in its own source, beyond every craving.
